title: cyanella alba subsp. alba
intro: Cyanella alba subsp. alba, also known as the White Sea Lily or Star Flower, is a plant species native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cyanella alba subsp. alba include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the flowers in hot water to create a tea, which can be used to relieve symptoms of colds and flu, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
